title: SE4 servant droid
content:

The '''SE4 servant droid''', also known as the '''SE4 Series Protocol Droid''', was a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] line constructed by [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]] to help with the planning of social occasions and parties.

==Description==
SE4 [[Droid/Legends|droids]] possessed a bipedal [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]] frame standing just under 1.6 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. Their heads contained standard [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] including a pair of [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]], two [[Audio sensor/Legends|auditory sensors]], and a [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]]. SE4s were programmed to understand around one hundred of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] most common [[Language/Legends|languages]], allowing them to function as [[Translator/Legends|translators]] in certain circumstances. The droids were also equipped with two sensitive [[Olfactory sensor/Legends|olfactory sensors]]; the primary sensor was located at the droid's collar, while the secondary was located on the unit's face and doubled as a cosmetic "nose." The left index finger was lined with simulated tastebuds for the proper preparation of recipes.<ref name="EGTD" />

The SE4 [[Service droid/Legends|servant droids]] was programmed with [[Knowledge matrix|knowledge matrices]] to be an expert in several fields relating to domestic service. Secondary [[Modular circuit matrix|skill modules]] could be installed in the droid through a slot on the droid's chest, broadening their range of knowledge and tailoring their skills to be of more use to their owners. The personality of an SE4 droid was uptight, even more so than a standard protocol droid. They were considered to be meticulous, but also persnickety, fretful, and were internally horrified to see breaches of etiquette being made which could cause the droid to shut off its own central processor. New SE4s retailed for around twenty-six hundred [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] on the open market,<ref name="EGTD" /> while used models could be purchased for thirteen hundred  credits.<ref name="CFTD" />

==Role==
SE4 servant droids were designed to perform domestic duties,<ref name="EGTD" /> serving in the role of a [[butler]] and general household aid with specialities in cooking and entertaining.<ref name="AEG" /> As such, they were programmed to be experts at party planning, home economics, the culinary arts, and even [[Flower/Legends|flower]] arranging. SE4s were well versed in minutiae such as knowing that diners should not use a three-pronged fork while eating [[bruallki]], that steam pockets needed to be released from [[boiled suuri]] by cracking the shell, and that one was never to serve chilled [[Algarian wine]] with [[plicto steak]].<ref name="EGTD" />

With the addition of extra skill modules, SE4s could take on a wider array of knowledge bases, ranging from more detailed knowledge of the local cuisine, to the ability to manage the maintenance for a palatial estate, or to operate as cleaners, handymen,<ref name="EGTD" /> or even babysitters.<ref name="AEG" /> SE4 droids were fully capable of performing minor repair tasks, but complicated work was still best left to dedicated [[Maintenance droid/Legends|maintenance droids]].<ref name="EGTD" />

==History==
The SE4 servant droid was one of Industrial Automaton's oldest product lines,<ref name="EGTD" /> and was first manufactured before the company began producing their [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] line. Originally marketed towards the wealthy, the SE4 soon fell into general use and was a common sight in lower-income homes as the [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] passed.<ref name="AEG" /> Throughout its lifecycle, the model proved to be resilient, and sales were not affected by the changing tastes of buyers or the ever-shifting galactic economy. As long as SE4s were being bought, Industrial Automaton continued to manufacture them. As the droids were capable of operating for generations, learning new customs as they went, most buyers were happy with the droids and felt they got value for their money.<ref name="EGTD" />

An SE4 servant droid named [[R02-E]] was employed by [[Drayk]] in the [[Dim-U monastery/Legends|Dim-U monastery]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="GG7" />

==Behind the scenes==
The taste bud is placed in the left finger in ''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'', but ''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' states that it's inside the right finger.
